text: Highlight clip Jeff Yan: My Early Startup Failures and What I Learned On February 19, Hyperliquid founder Jeff Yan said in an interview with When Shift Happens that in 2018 he launched a prediction market project around the same time as Kalshi and others, but it ultimately did not succeed. He said the "off-chain matching, on-chain settlement" infrastructure direction was correct at the time, but ideas account for only a very, very small part - the real difficulty lies in achieving product–market fit. As the market shifted from bull to bear, users were no longer willing to try on-chain products. He added that the team learned a great deal through the cycle. Although the project did not succeed, he is glad to see peers from that period who persisted and are now gradually moving toward mass adoption. — link

text: Highlight clip CZ: My Mother Traveled 6 Hours to See Me in Prison, But Was Turned Away In an April 9 interview with Scott Melker, Binance founder Changpeng Zhao (CZ) shared that his prison experience made him value his family more. He was deeply moved when his mother made a 6-hour journey to visit him in prison, only to be turned away. CZ stated that he previously focused his energy entirely on his company, and it wasn't until he lost his freedom that he realized what he missed most wasn't material comforts, but his family. Since his release, he has significantly increased the time spent with his loved ones, adding that running a company isn't the only important thing in life having a good family is equally vital. — link

text: Aztec Team Transfers All ETH from Public AZTEC Auction to Coinbase Aztec sold 15% of its AZTEC tokens through a public auction last December, raising 19,388.4 ETH (~$59.13M) at roughly $0.0473 per token, now down around 50% from the sale price. At TGE, 4,234.6 ETH from the auction proceeds was allocated to build liquidity, while the remaining ~15,154 ETH has been fully transferred to Coinbase over the past three months, with the final transfer completed on April 17 at 16:44 UTC. H/T: @EmberCN — link

text: Highlight clip Arthur Hayes: 90% of My Net Worth Is Bitcoin On April 10, 2026, BitMEX Co-founder Arthur Hayes stated in Anthony Pompliano's interview that over 90% of his net worth is in Bitcoin. Given his low cost basis, he remains inactive amid market fluctuations. When discussing capital choices—deploying more fiat or selling some Bitcoin for faster-appreciating altcoins—he noted he is currently most bullish on Zcash and Hyperliquid. As for new fiat, he stated he will not put it into Bitcoin today, as he is still waiting for a major "money printing" event. — link
author: admin#1534592031

text: Base Early Marketer Joins Anthropic, the World's Most Valuable AI Startup Base's early core marketing lead @sarahzorah announced she is leaving Coinbase after nearly five years. She described joining as Coinbase's first "builder marketer" and working with Jesse Pollak to help launch and grow Base from 2023, spanning developer community building and brand partnerships. She said her next role will be at Anthropic, focusing on startup marketing and supporting the ecosystem being built around Claude. — link

text: Coinbase Ventures: Funding Slows, Capital Shifts to Tokenization, Trading Infrastructure, Next-Gen DeFi, AI Agents Coinbase Ventures investor Jonathan King said that while crypto startup funding fell year over year in Q1 2026, the market has become more builder-driven, with capital concentrating in four areas: tokenization, more specialized trading venues and market infrastructure, next-generation DeFi focused on capital efficiency and composability, where privacy could be a key unlock, and AI agents as economic actors driving demand for onchain payments and automated trading. — link
author: admin#1534592031

text: Zcash Ships Security Updates to Fix Node-Crash, Consensus-Split Risks Zcash has released zcashd v6.12.1 and the Zcash Foundation has released Zebra v4.3.1, patching four vulnerabilities, including an Orchard action-encoding issue that could crash nodes and a related risk of consensus divergence between the two clients. Mining pools running both implementations have already deployed the fixes, and developers noted no evidence of exploitation, with user funds and privacy unaffected and no ZEC supply inflation possible; users are advised to upgrade. — link
author: admin#1534592031
